meta.title: Safety Culture Assessment — {Organisation / Site / Unit}
meta.subtitle: Maturity model + Schein lens, with advancement roadmap
meta.author: HSE Leadership
meta.deid_notice: All respondents are referred to by role/group/cohort label only. No cohort breakdown of fewer than 5 respondents is published; secondary suppression is applied; no verbatim quote identifies a speaker. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This assessment diagnoses the safety culture of the named unit against a recognised maturity model (and/or the Schein three-levels lens), triangulated across at least two data sources. It is a culture DIAGNOSIS — not a rating from a single survey — and drives a systemic advancement roadmap. Decision-support only; a competent person must review it before use.

items: Model / lens: {Bradley | Hudson | Westrum | Schein three-levels | combined} — cited as method, not law (source + year quoted)
Data sources (>=2, triangulated): {validated culture survey} + {leadership-walk / observation data} + {records}
Confidentiality threshold: cohort cells <5 suppressed (secondary suppression); paraphrase-only quotes

sections.caption: Maturity banding — each dimension triangulated across >=2 sources; no single-survey rating; no <5 cohort cell published

rows: {what actually wins when schedule vs safety collide}
sections.caption: Schein three-levels diagnosis — named espoused-vs-enacted gaps, NOT a maturity band; standalone Schein is a diagnosis, not a rating

rows.title: {systemic driver}
rows.description: What in the leadership system / work design holds the culture where it is — attributed to a role/group/system, never an individual.

rows.control: Close the named espoused-vs-enacted gap: fix the consequence system so the safe choice is the easy choice

sections.text: Advancement roadmap (SMART, tracked to leading indicators)

rows.action: {advancement move tied to a leading indicator from KB-DATA-LEADING-INDICATORS}
{advancement move — measured by consultation cadence / walk-commitment closure-rate}

sections.text: This is a culture diagnosis. Operationalise it with the sibling skills: bbs-program-designer (observation programme), safety-walk-gemba (leadership walks), and leading-lagging-kpi-framework (the balanced KPI set that tracks advancement).

items: Role/group/cohort labels only; no <5 cohort cell; no identifying verbatim quote; no individual named as the culture problem
Culture models cited as method, not law (Bradley / Hudson / Westrum / Schein)
ISO 45001 5.1 (leadership & commitment) and 5.4 (consultation & participation of workers) — the duties this assessment evidences
Decision-support only; a competent person must review this output
